name: Carnaval de Granville
dates: 13-17 February 2026 (5 days around Shrove Tuesday annually)
description: One of the oldest and most important carnivals in France. First organized edition: February 7, 1875, linked to the city's maritime history and departure of cod fishermen for Newfoundland. UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity since 2016 — the only French carnival with this recognition. Attracts ~130,000 spectators (largest carnival in Western France). Program includes Children's Day, main parades with ~40 satirical floats, 'Intrigues' (unique tradition where masked participants accost passers-by), Carnival Judgment/burning, balls and brass bands. 152nd edition in 2026.

name: Carnaval de Nice
description: One of the three largest carnivals in the world alongside Rio and Venice. Organized in modern form since 1873. Place Masséna and Promenade des Anglais host parades of monumental floats, spectacular 'batailles de fleurs' (flower battles), and illuminated parades (corsos illuminés). Each edition has a different theme with King Carnival reigning. Attracts over 1 million spectators. 153rd edition in 2026, theme: 'Vive la Reine!'. Tickets from €16 (standing) to €45 (package).

name: Carnaval de Dunkerque (Dunkirk Carnival)
description: One of the most famous carnivals in France. Origins in 17th century: shipowners celebrated fishermen leaving for Iceland for 6 months. From January to April, balls, parades ('bandes'), and rigadoons animate the city. The highlight 'Trois Joyeuses' features 450 kg of herrings thrown from the Town Hall balcony to tens of thousands of disguised revelers. Herring toss on Sunday 15 February at 5 PM. Season spans Dunkirk and neighboring towns (Malo-les-Bains, Rosendaël, Coudekerque-Branche, Saint-Pol-sur-Mer).

name: Carnaval de Jargeau
dates: 15 February – 27 June 2026 (winter: 15 & 22 Feb; summer night parade: 27 Jun)
description: One of the most famous carnivals in France and the largest in the Loiret. Existing since the 19th century, adopts a different theme each year since 1946. Festivities span 8-9 days around Mardi Gras, with two winter parades featuring bands, confetti battles, and the burning of King Carnival on the Loire banks. 2026 theme: 'Cult Films'. Since 2001, an illuminated summer parade attracts over 10,000 spectators.

name: Carnaval Biarnès
description: Created in 1985. The largest winter festival in Aquitaine and one of the five most important carnivals in mainland France. 2026 theme: 'SAUNEJAR!'. Takes place in the streets of Pau's city center. Completely free.

name: Carnaval de Limoux
dates: 10 January – 22 March 2026 (every weekend, January to March annually)
description: One of the longest carnivals in the world — runs every Saturday and Sunday from January to March. Takes place on Place de la République and in the historic center of Limoux. Features masked processions and the 'Nuit de la Blanquette' (ticketed special event). Free entry for street parades.

name: Carnaval de Cholet
dates: 18-25 April 2026 (day parade 19 Apr; night parade 25 Apr)
description: Since 1906, one of the most important festive events in the Grand Ouest. Organized by the Cholet carnival associations (notably System'D). Combines artistic creativity, local traditions, and a funfair. Cholet is the 'capital of carnival' in Maine-et-Loire. Day parade 18 April 2:30-6 PM; night parade 25 April 9:30 PM-1 AM. Also Carnaval Guinguette, Carnival for Leisure Centers, and Carnival Cycling Grand Prix.

name: Carnaval d'Hagondange
description: One of the most important carnivals in Eastern France (Moselle), attracting ~40,000 people per edition. Created in 1985. Grand international parade with dozens of decorated floats, brass bands, costumed groups from France and abroad, Queen of the Carnival ball, and children's carnival. 42nd edition in 2026. Located 20 km north of Metz.

name: Carnaval de Belfort
description: Popular spring event in the Territoire de Belfort. Every year in March, colorful floats from the Festival Committee, socio-cultural centers, and neighborhood houses parade through city streets accompanied by brass bands. Concludes with traditional burning of Monsieur Carnaval. Completely free.

name: Fontenay-le-Comte Carnival
description: One of the biggest popular festivals in southern Vendée. Transforms the streets of the former capital of Bas-Poitou with elaborate floats, brass bands, costumed groups. Concludes with traditional judgment and burning of Monsieur Carnaval. Intergenerational festival with Rich Renaissance heritage.

name: Carnival of Saint-Laurent-de-Cerdans (Bear Festival)
dates: 25 February – 1 March 2026 (annually in late February/early March)
description: Ancestral traditional festival in Haut-Vallespir at the foot of the Canigou massif. Intimately linked to the Bear Festival, inscribed on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage. Perpetuates the oldest Catalan traditions. 2026: masked balls, collective meal (27 Feb), and the spectacular bear hunt (1 Mar at 10 AM).

name: Carnaval de Montpellier
description: Carnival parade in the streets of Montpellier with floats, music, and entertainment. Since 1894. 133rd edition in 2026. Free entry.

name: Carnaval de Bastia
description: Carnival in Bastia, Haute-Corse, Corsica. Created in 1910. 117th edition in 2026. A must-attend cultural event for the municipality and the entire Haute-Corse department. Free entry.

name: Carnaval Tropical de Paris
description: Created in 2001. The largest tropical parade in the French capital. Over 4,000 dancers and musicians from the Antilles, Guyana, Reunion Island, Brazil, Colombia, and other countries parade up the Champs-Élysées. Organized by the Federation of Carnaval Tropical de Paris. Celebrates cultural diversity and carnival traditions of overseas territories and tropical regions. Attracts hundreds of thousands of spectators. Completely free.

name: Carnaval Vénitien de Remiremont
description: The oldest Venetian carnival in France, founded in 1996 by Yves Chrétien. Takes place in the abbey district and town center. Features Venetian market on Rue de la Franche-Pierre. All events completely free.

name: Carnaval Vénitien de Verdun
description: Major festive event in Verdun, nicknamed 'Little Venice' for its five canals and twenty bridges. Features costumed parades, commedia dell'arte shows, boat trips on the Meuse (€6 adults, €4 children), and a Venetian market. Organized by the Union des Commerçants de Verdun. Costumed participants must wear a traditional Venetian costume. Free admission to parades.

name: Carnaval de Bourg-la-Reine
description: Every spring, Bourg-la-Reine revives its carnival tradition with a colorful parade winding through the town's streets. Free entry for all.

name: Carnaval d'Alençon
description: Annual festive event revived in 2024. Unfolds over three days: school parades on Thursday and Friday, grand procession open to all on Saturday. Decorated floats, brass bands, dance groups, DJ sets, and street entertainment. 2026 theme: imaginary characters. Free and open to all.

name: Maîche Carnival
description: One of the largest and most popular carnivals in eastern France (Haut-Doubs). Brings together thousands of people each year. 2026 theme: 'Ça va cartooner!'. Grand parade in main streets with burning of the Bonhomme Carnaval in Place de la Rasse. Free admission to parade and Friday concerts.

name: Venice Carnival Weekend at Château de Valençay
description: Festive and elegant event at the Château de Valençay transporting visitors to the atmosphere of the Venetian carnival. Grand procession daily at 3 PM. Château entrance fee applies (€14.50 full price, €11.50 reduced).

name: Venetian Parade of Riquewihr
description: Venetian-themed festival in the historic village of Riquewihr on the Alsace Wine Route. Venetian market (9 AM-6 PM), multiple parades, artistic makeup, costume presentation, nocturnal parade. Free admission.
